_comment: Per-model window framing, read by the renderer (index.html). scale = zoom multiplier on the fit (1.0 = fit whole model; >1 crops in to upper body). centerY = model centre as a fraction of window height (higher = model sits lower on screen = more headroom above the head for the speech bubble). This model is a tall full-body rig, so it is zoomed to the upper body and pushed down to clear the bubble. tears = how she cries: 'sprite' draws falling tear drops overlaid on the model (the only way to get slow FALLING tears; her native ParamTear renders nothing visible), positioned + sized by tearTune below; 'native'/'none' draw no overlay. tearTune (sprite only) is measured for THIS rig: eyeL/eyeR = horizontal tear-spawn offset from model centre as a fraction of displayed width (asymmetric because her face is off-centre); eyeDY = vertical offset from centre (very negative because a full-body rig puts the eyes far above model centre); fall = how far a drop falls before fading (fraction of height); gentleSize/crySize = drop size (fraction of height, small because her face is a small part of the full-body frame). hideDrawables = a per-feeling map { feelingName: [drawableIDs] } of blush pieces to force-hide every frame WHILE that feeling is active. ParamTere draws this rig's blush as three separate drawables (D_PSD_65 / _66 / _69); blushing (no entry) shows all three for a full blush, while happy hides D_PSD_65 so its merry flush shows only the two cheek pieces (66 + 69).
